label: PostgreSQL
description: Connect to a PostgreSQL database to run queries, read tables, and write data.

form.title: Connect PostgreSQL
form.subtitle: Provide your connection details to let Anton query your PostgreSQL database.

methods.label: Connection string
methods.description: Paste a single PostgreSQL connection URI (postgres://user:password@host:port/database). Quickest option if you already have one handy.
methods.how_to:
## Connect via connection string

A PostgreSQL connection URI bundles all your credentials into one string. Most cloud providers (Supabase, Render, Railway, Neon, etc.) show a ready-to-copy URI in their dashboard.

### Where to find it

1. **Supabase**: Project → Settings → Database → Connection string → URI tab.
2. **Render**: Service dashboard → Info → Connection → Internal or External database URL.
3. **Railway**: Project → your Postgres plugin → Connect → Postgres Connection URL.
4. **Neon**: Project → Connection Details → pick the branch → copy the URI.
5. For self-hosted instances, construct it manually:
   `postgres://USER:PASSWORD@HOST:PORT/DATABASE`

### Notes

- The URI must use the `postgres://` or `postgresql://` scheme.
- URL-encode special characters in the password (e.g. `@` → `%40`).
- Append `?sslmode=require` to the URI to enforce SSL, or `?sslmode=disable` to turn it off.
- Anton stores the URI encrypted at rest; the password is never shown again after saving.

fields.name: connection_uri
fields.label: Connection URI
fields.description: Full PostgreSQL connection URI. Append ?sslmode=require to enforce SSL.

methods.label: Host / port / credentials
methods.description: Enter host, port, database name, username, and password individually. Supports SSL toggle. Best when you don't have a ready-made connection URI.
methods.how_to:
## Connect with host, port, and credentials

Fill in each field separately. This matches the connection panel in tools like pgAdmin, DBeaver, or TablePlus.

### Where to find each value

1. **Host**: The server address — e.g. `db.example.com` or an IP. Cloud providers show this on their database info page.
2. **Port**: Almost always `5432`. Change only if your DBA or cloud provider specifies otherwise.
3. **Database**: The specific database name (not the server). In Supabase this is `postgres` by default.
4. **Username / Password**: The Postgres role credentials. For cloud providers, check the database "Connection details" or "Credentials" panel.
5. **SSL**: Toggle on if your server requires SSL (recommended for any internet-facing database). Most cloud-hosted Postgres instances require it.

### Notes

- If connecting to a local instance, use `localhost` or `127.0.0.1` as the host and leave SSL off.
- Make sure the host/port is reachable from your machine — firewalls and VPCs often block external access.
- The user needs at minimum `CONNECT` + `SELECT` privileges on the target database.
- For write operations, also grant `INSERT`, `UPDATE`, and `DELETE` as needed.

fields.name: host
fields.label: Host
fields.description: Hostname or IP address of your PostgreSQL server.

fields.name: port
fields.label: Port
fields.description: PostgreSQL listens on 5432 by default. Change only if your server uses a custom port.

fields.name: database
fields.label: Database name
fields.description: The name of the specific database (schema container) to connect to.

fields.name: user
fields.label: Username
fields.description: PostgreSQL role / user that Anton will authenticate as.

fields.name: password
fields.label: Password
fields.description: Password for the above user. Stored encrypted and never shown again.

fields.name: ssl_enabled
fields.label: Require SSL
fields.description: Enable to enforce an encrypted connection. Recommended for any internet-facing server.
